title: Personal and Shared Knowledge
creator: Dan McCreary
subject: Theory of Knowledge
description: An interactive infographic examining the bidirectional relationship between personal and shared knowledge. Students click example cards to see narratives about knowledge flow, add their own examples, and toggle mechanism labels on directional arrows.

learningObjectives: Examine the two-directional relationship between personal and shared knowledge by tracing how knowledge moves between the individual and the community
prerequisites: Familiarity with the TOK distinction between personal and shared knowledge

keyQuestions: Is any knowledge purely personal or purely shared?
What mechanisms transform personal knowledge into shared knowledge?
How does shared knowledge become personal for each individual?
commonMisconceptions: Personal and shared knowledge are completely separate categories
Knowledge only flows in one direction (from shared to personal)
Personal knowledge is less valid than shared knowledge
assessmentOpportunities: Identification of edge cases that blur the boundary
